hooks.command: branch=$(git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD 2>/dev/null | sed 's|refs/remotes/origin/||'); branch=${branch:-main}; if git status --porcelain | grep -q .; then printf '%s' '{"systemMessage":"DIRTY WORKING TREE — surface to user before any work.","hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"Working tree is dirty. Do NOT start any task. Run git status immediately and ask the user how to handle it (stash, commit, or abandon changes)."}}'; else git checkout "$branch" 2>/dev/null; git pull --ff-only 2>/dev/null; printf '{"systemMessage":"Session ready: on %s, up to date.","hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"Session initialized: checked out %s and pulled latest. Remember: create a feature branch before making any code changes."}}' "$branch" "$branch"; fi

jq -c 'if .tool_input.subagent_type == "cara-dune" then {hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", additionalContext: "WATCH LIST when reviewing cara-dune output: (1) Did it write anything under .claude/agent-memory/cara-dune/? If not, `memory: project` is not auto-injecting — add a short memory pointer back to the agent body (not the full preamble). (2) Did it run `uv run --extra dev pytest`? If it fell back to bare `pytest`, tighten rule 5 in .claude/agents/cara-dune.md to make CLAUDE.md read mandatory. (3) Did its output include the `OVERALL COVERAGE: <int>%` line on its own line? ig-11 parses this exactly."}} else empty end'
